reasoning: The finding is confirmed. The AJAX handler mk_dp_close_dp_help() is registered only under wp_ajax_ (line 44), so unauthenticated users are correctly excluded. Any logged-in WordPress user (subscriber and above) can obtain a valid nc_help_desk nonce from any admin page that enqueues the plugin's custom_assets() script (line 434), then POST to admin-ajax.php?action=mk_dp_close_dp_help&nonce=<nonce>. The handler (lines 408-425) verifies the nonce but performs no current_user_can() check before calling update_option('mk_fm_close_fm_help_c', 'done'). This permanently suppresses the admin help-desk/rate-us notice for all administrators. No additional guards or sanitizers are present. The reachability claim is accurate. The severity is low because the sole impact is hiding a cosmetic promotional banner; there is no data exfiltration, privilege escalation, or content modification.
